summary: This brief post humorously acknowledges the unspoken reality of tech-savvy people returning home for Thanksgiving: the holiday visit is really about providing IT support for family members. Atwood quotes a Newsweek article that captures this universal experience of the technically competent being drafted into fixing parents' computers. The post is essentially a knowing nod to a shared experience among developers and tech workers.
key_points: Thanksgiving homecomings for tech workers inevitably involve computer repair duties
The gap between family expectations (warm holiday gathering) and reality (tech support) is a universal developer experience
Technical competence comes with unwritten family obligations
money_quotes: We are going home to fix our parents' computers.
key_insight: Being the family's tech person means holidays come with an implicit IT support contract.
